QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_24

question.text: What is a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP)?

answer.text: A method of investing a fixed amount at regular intervals (monthly/quarterly) in a mutual fund
A one-time lump sum investment in a mutual fund
A type of insurance policy linked to mutual funds
A government scheme that guarantees monthly returns

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) allows you to invest a fixed amount (e.g., Rs. 1,000) at regular intervals (usually monthly) into a mutual fund scheme. It instills financial discipline and benefits from rupee cost averaging — buying more units when NAV is low and fewer when NAV is high. **The Trap:** SIP is NOT a one-time investment (that is called a lump sum). It is also NOT insurance and NOT a government scheme. SIP is simply a method of investing regularly. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under investment methods. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SIP is your best tool for clients who want to invest but are afraid of market timing. Tell them: "You do not need to worry about when to invest — SIP automatically invests for you every month, rain or shine."
